evidence_snippets: SRS will cover the Space Fractions product end-to-end
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes call the document the SRS for product.

evidence_snippets: Andrea Brooks from Pecan Springs Elementary
coverage_entries.reason: Requester named explicitly in transcript and notes.

evidence_snippets: SRS structure: Use the component template
coverage_entries.reason: Notes enumerate purpose, interfaces, components, requirements.

evidence_snippets: SRS structure: Use the component template
Inputs: user clicks, browser environment
coverage_entries.reason: Template required but legal domains and parameter ranges absent.

evidence_snippets: Audience: sixth-graders and teachers
coverage_entries.reason: Notes and transcript specify sixth graders and teacher.

evidence_snippets: Runs in a browser; no installs on lab PCs
coverage_entries.reason: Notes state web delivery and no permanent client files.

evidence_snippets: Implementation: Macromedia Flash (v5) + HTML
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes and emails specify Flash+HTML and linked movies.

evidence_snippets: needs JavaScript and the Flash plug-in
coverage_entries.reason: Notes require browser support for JavaScript and Flash 5.

evidence_snippets: Runs on any Internet-capable computer; no new hardware
coverage_entries.reason: Sources state no new hardware and no mic/camera access.

evidence_snippets: standalone in the browser. It’s not embedded
coverage_entries.reason: Notes require umbrella interface of independent Flash movies.

evidence_snippets: program logic must behave the same
coverage_entries.reason: Notes/emails require consistent behavior across browsers.

evidence_snippets: core is fraction problems
coverage_entries.reason: Artifacts repeatedly state a fractions-focused educational game.

evidence_snippets: multiple-choice items with cartoon panels
coverage_entries.reason: Transcript/notes describe plot-based cartoon MC fraction questions.

evidence_snippets: multiple choice with clickable options—no keyboard
coverage_entries.reason: Sources mandate clickable multiple-choice and mouse-only input.

evidence_snippets: show clear "correct" indicator then advance
coverage_entries.reason: Notes and transcript specify correct feedback then auto-advance.

evidence_snippets: First attempt wrong does not count as full correct
coverage_entries.reason: Documents require retry and scoring that counts first-try only.

evidence_snippets: Robot sidekick: provides context-sensitive hints
coverage_entries.reason: Interview and notes specify a robot giving contextual hints.

evidence_snippets: branch ending based on flagged critical questions
coverage_entries.reason: Materials describe branching at critical questions to pick endings.

evidence_snippets: After N questions (N configurable), route to ending
coverage_entries.reason: Notes state configurable question count then route to ending.

evidence_snippets: ignore inputs other than mouse clicks
coverage_entries.reason: Intro is skippable by click; non-mouse inputs ignored per notes.

evidence_snippets: Main title screen provides: Begin Game, Help, team blurb
Wait state: do nothing until user chooses
coverage_entries.reason: Source describes menu, team link, Denominators and wait state.

evidence_snippets: Begin Game takes the user directly
Begin Game takes the user directly into the game sequence.
coverage_entries.reason: Docs state Begin launches the game sequence.

evidence_snippets: Link out to Denominators.
New window for those external ones.
coverage_entries.reason: Denominators link present and opens in a new window.

evidence_snippets: Help: basic instructions
Help screen—we’ll keep it simple
coverage_entries.reason: Help screen from main menu with basic gameplay instructions.

evidence_snippets: End screen shows exact score + plot-based conclusion
Try Again/Main Menu/Exit
coverage_entries.reason: End screen shows exact score, story ending, and those options.

evidence_snippets: score maintained only in session
Scoring maintained only within the session. Replay resets score.
coverage_entries.reason: Notes explicitly state session-local score and end-of-game presentation.

evidence_snippets: Outputs: visual screens, sound cues
Implementation: Macromedia Flash (v5) + HTML
coverage_entries.reason: Flash movies and sound/animation outputs are specified.

evidence_snippets: Related Projects (fractions, decimals, percents)
open in a new window
coverage_entries.reason: Related project links are listed and open in separate windows.

evidence_snippets: forms (pulldowns + text fields)
generate a text file on the game server
coverage_entries.reason: Updater web form, validation, and server text-file generation described.

evidence_snippets: customize per-choice hints/feedback
context-sensitive hints tied to choices
coverage_entries.reason: Admins can edit per-choice hints and customize feedback.

evidence_snippets: Hosted on the S2S website
coverage_entries.reason: Hosted on S2S site; internet-accessible for multiple students.

evidence_snippets: Flash game modularized into scenes/sub-scenes
coverage_entries.reason: Flash game modularized into scenes/sub-scenes for maintainability.

evidence_snippets: Security model matches standard browser/hosting context
coverage_entries.reason: Security model tied to standard browser/hosting context.

evidence_snippets: thorough testing by team members; mentors
coverage_entries.reason: Notes require thorough testing by team members and mentor review.

unsupported_implied.text: The updater (web-accessible question editor) shall be access-controlled (password-protected) so that only designated editors/admins can modify game content.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: Access to the updater page will be protected with a password so only designated editors can change content (Jenna to coordinate setup).
unsupported_implied.reason: The source explicitly requires password protection for the updater tool, a concrete authentication/access-control requirement for administrative actions. The gold set includes a general note that security is provided by the browser environment but does not specify updater access control or require password protection for admin/editor functions, so this is a significant capability not covered by the gold requirements.
